title: Region inference '90% of code needs no annotations' claim is unsubstantiated
description: The spec claims region inference eliminates annotations in at least 90% of practical code, but provides no empirical data, no definition of 'practical code', and no guidance for the remaining 10%. When inference fails, the error message does not suggest which signature patterns would make it succeed. Developers who hit a region inference failure have no path forward except reading deep compiler documentation. Either: (a) measure the claim against a real code corpus and publish the methodology, (b) replace the percentage with a qualitative description, or (c) provide clear documentation of which borrow patterns require manual annotations and what the annotations look like.
close_reason: Replaced unsubstantiated 90% claim with qualitative three-category breakdown plus aspirational quote and design decision note. Updated all three locations.
